page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, it bundles a 2500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering high-capacity, low-latency local storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7ie.xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-threaded performance, with its Geekbench single-core score and single-threaded gzip compression rates placing in the top 10% of tested instances. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, falls into the bottom 25%, reflecting the physical core constraint of a 2-core configuration. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is also relatively weak, though small-block cached reads and large-block writes maintain average performance.

Given its high ratio of storage and memory relative to its compute footprint, the i7ie.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require substantial local storage and memory capacity without demanding massive parallel CPU processing. It is highly suited for database operations, caching layers, single-threaded text processing, and lightweight data-serving applications where local NVMe storage and DDR5 memory speeds are critical.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a bundled 2500 GB NVMe SSD, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The server exhibits top-tier single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing benchmarks. However, its multi-core processing capabilities are limited, as shown by lower-tier multi-threaded stress-ng and bzip3 decompression results. With a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps and support for advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the i7ie.xlarge is well-suited for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads that do not require high parallel compute scaling, such as local database hosting, caching systems, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for database and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2500 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining high-speed DDR5 memory and a massive local NVMe SSD for data-intensive, single-core prioritized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
Bundled 2500 GB local NVMe SSD for high-capacity, low-latency storage
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core performance in gzip compression and text processing
Best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and single-threaded tasks
